notes.title: raintree-forest-sg-long

frontmatter.project_name: Raintree Forest Sungai Long — Infrastructure Cost Comparison (All Phases)
frontmatter.report_reference: Project 4236 — Raintree Forest Sg Long (Based on CCE 2A), dated 2 Oct 2025
frontmatter.scope: Infrastructure cost (all phases, Oct 2025) + Building superstructure cost by unit type (DCS 19B / CCE No 2 / CCE No 2A, Nov 2024); full development cost estimate excluding land, professional fees, S&M, financing

notes.content:
# Raintree Forest Sungai Long — Infrastructure Cost Comparison

## Project Overview

Infrastructure cost comparison report for the Raintree Forest landed housing development in Sungai Long (Kajang, Selangor). Sungai Long is an established township ~30 km south-east of KL city centre in Hulu Langat district.

This document compares **7 phase/reference datasets** within the same development, allowing direct apples-to-apples comparison of how terrain type, phase scale, and density affect infrastructure cost per unit and per sqft GFA.

> ⚠️ **Scope note:** These are **infrastructure costs only** — roads, earthworks, slope works, drainage, sewerage, and water supply. Building superstructure (the houses themselves) is a separate cost not captured here. GFA figures refer to the gross floor area of the residential units; they are used as the cost denominator (RM/sqft GFA), not as built-up floor area of infrastructure.

---

## Phase / Reference Summary

| Ref | Phase | Units | Land (acres) | Density (u/a) | GFA (sqft) | GFA/unit (sqft) | Plot Ratio | Terrain |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Phase 1 | Raintree Phase 1 | 106 | 26.760 | 4 | 423,326 | 3,994 | 0.36 | Hilly ~30m |
| Phase 2 | Raintree Phase 2 | 156 | 22.430 | 7 | 571,420 | 3,663 | 0.59 | Hilly ~30m |
| P1+P2 Total | Phase 1 & 2 Combined | 262 | 49.190 | 5 | 994,746 | 3,797 | 0.46 | Hilly |
| MBW Ph1&2 | MBW Phase 1&2 | 324 | 18.53 | 17 | 964,898 | 2,977 | 1.20 | — |
| MBW | MBW | 222 | 13.82 | 16 | 672,259 | 3,028 | 1.12 | — |
| Main | Main (large township ref) | 2,091 | 108.47 | 19 | 3,611,381 | 1,727 | 0.76 | Flat |
| Phase 5A&5B | Phase 5A & 5B | 58 | 11.05 | 5 | 226,578 | 3,906 | 0.47 | Hilly |

> MBW = likely terrace/semi-D phases; Phase 5A&5B likely larger semi-D/bungalow. Main is a large township-scale reference (flat ground).

---

## Cost Summary — Within Site Boundary (WWSB)

| Item | Ph 1 (RM) | Ph 1 (RM/ft²) | Ph 2 (RM) | Ph 2 (RM/ft²) | P1+P2 (RM) | P1+P2 (RM/ft²) | MBW P1&2 (RM/ft²) | MBW (RM/ft²) | Main (RM/ft²) | Ph5A&5B (RM/ft²) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 Demolition | — | — | — | — | — | — | — | 0.45 | — | — |
| 2 Earthworks | 534,547 | 1.26 | 3,749,010 | 6.56 | 4,283,557 | 4.31 | 2.83 | 4.99 | 1.27 | 3.14 |
| 3 Slope Stabilization | — | — | — | — | — | — | — | — | 0.20 | — |
| 4 Detention Pond | — | — | — | — | — | — | — | — | 0.16 | — |
| 5 Retaining Walls | 944,527 | 2.23 | 1,324,923 | 2.32 | 2,269,449 | 2.28 | 0.08 | 5.78 | 0.85 | 18.26 |
| 6 Roads, Kerbs & Footpath | 4,716,108 | 11.14 | 3,910,504 | 6.84 | 8,626,612 | 8.67 | 1.23 | 5.45 | 7.03 | 10.67 |
| 7 Surface Water Drainage | 5,526,703 | 13.06 | 4,996,874 | 8.74 | 10,523,577 | 10.58 | 5.16 | 5.81 | 3.39 | 7.69 |
| 8 Foul Drainage | 2,129,414 | 5.03 | 2,112,109 | 3.70 | 4,241,523 | 4.26 | 0.73 | 2.09 | 1.28 | 2.67 |
| 9 Sewerage Treatment Plant | 2,179,987 | 5.15 | — | — | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| 10 Sewerage Force Main | — | — | — | — | — | — | — | — | 0.12 | — |
| 11 Sewerage Pipe Jacking | — | — | — | — | — | — | — | — | 0.12 | — |
| 12 Sewerage Pump Station | — | — | — | — | — | — | — | — | 0.19 | — |
| 13 Water Reticulation | 1,440,364 | 3.40 | 1,692,358 | 2.96 | 3,132,722 | 3.15 | 0.76 | 1.66 | 1.55 | 2.53 |
| 14 Water Reservoir | — | — | — | — | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| 15 Hardscape Works | — | — | — | — | — | — | 2.05 | 3.35 | — | — |
| 16 Fencing & Gate | 478,851 | 1.13 | 205,976 | 0.36 | 684,827 | 0.69 | 0.76 | 1.28 | — | 0.98 |
| 17 Geotechnical Instrumentation | — | — | — | — | — | — | — | 0.34 | — | — |
| **Total WWSB** | **17,950,502** | **42.40** | **17,991,753** | **31.49** | **33,762,267** | **33.94** | **13.58** | **31.21** | **16.15** | **45.94** |

> Drainage specs by phase: Phase 1 — RC sumps, 750mm–1,500mm pipe, box culvert, brick U-drain. Phase 2 — OSD, brick sumps, 600mm–1,050mm pipe. MBW P1&2 — OSD, brick & RC sumps, 300–700mm pipe, RC cut-off drain. Main — RC sumps, OSD, 900mm pipe, 600mm–3,000mm RC PC U-drain. Phase 5A&5B — OSD, RC sumps, 900mm pipe, cascading brick/RC drains.

---

## Cost Summary — Outside Site Boundary (WOSB)

| Item | P1+P2 (RM/ft²) | MBW P1&2 (RM/ft²) | MBW (RM/ft²) | Main (RM/ft²) | Ph5A&5B (RM/ft²) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 Retaining Walls | — | 1.49 | — | 0.29 | — |
| 2 Roads | 1.98 | 0.61 | 0.08 | — | 3.62 |
| 3 Surface Water | 1.06 | 0.52 | — | — | 2.19 |
| 4 Foul Drainage | — | 0.47 | — | — | 1.39 |
| 5 Water Reticulation | 1.09 | 0.25 | 0.30 | 1.06 | 0.97 |
| 6 Elevated Water Tank | 2.08 | — | — | 2.50 | — |
| 7 Sewerage Treatment Plant | 2.19 | — | — | 1.07 | — |
| 8 Detention Pond | — | — | — | 0.12 | — |
| 9 Slope Stabilization | — | — | — | 0.16 | — |
| **Total WOSB** | **4.13** | **3.34** | **0.38** | **1.35** | **8.18** |

---

## Grand Total (WWSB + WOSB)

| Phase | Total (MYR) | RM/sqft GFA | RM/acre | RM/unit |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Phase 1 (hilly, 106u, 4u/a) | 17,950,502 | **42.40** | 670,796 | **169,344** |
| Phase 2 (hilly, 156u, 7u/a) | 17,991,753 | **31.49** | 802,129 | **115,332** |
| Total Phase 1+2 (hilly, 262u) | 37,874,639 | **38.07** | 769,966 | **144,560** |
| MBW Phase 1&2 (324u, 18.53ac) | 16,327,767 | **16.92** | 881,153 | **50,394** |
| MBW (222u, 13.82ac) | 21,239,422 | **31.59** | 1,536,861 | **95,673** |
| Main (2,091u, 108.47ac, flat) | 63,187,300 | **17.50** | 582,533 | **30,216** |
| Phase 5A&5B (hilly, 58u, 5u/a) | 12,262,566 | **54.12** | 1,109,734 | **211,424** |

---

## Cost Benchmarks & Key Observations

### 1. Terrain multiplier — Hilly vs Flat

| Metric | Hilly (Phase 1) | Flat (Main) | Premium |
|---|---|---|---|
| Total infra RM/sqft GFA | 42.40 | 17.50 | **+142%** |
| Earthworks RM/sqft GFA | 1.26 | 1.27 | Similar |
| Retaining Walls RM/sqft GFA | 2.23 | 0.85 | **+162%** |
| Surface Water Drainage RM/sqft GFA | 13.06 | 3.39 | **+285%** |
| Roads RM/sqft GFA | 11.14 | 7.03 | +58% |

> The biggest cost driver in hilly terrain is **surface water drainage** — managing hill runoff requires significantly larger pipe sizes, OSD tanks, and more complex drainage systems. Retaining walls add a further material premium.

### 2. Scale effect — Cost per unit by phase size

| Phase | Units | RM/unit |
|---|---|---|
| Phase 5A&5B | 58 | 211,424 |
| Phase 1 | 106 | 169,344 |
| Phase 2 | 156 | 115,332 |
| MBW | 222 | 95,673 |
| MBW Ph1&2 | 324 | 50,394 |
| Main | 2,091 | 30,216 |

> Infrastructure cost per unit falls steeply with scale. A 58-unit phase costs **7× more per unit** than a 2,091-unit township. The curve is steep up to ~300 units, then flattens.

### 3. STP (Sewerage Treatment Plant)

- Phase 1 carries its own on-site STP (MYR 2.18M) — unusual; likely required by DOE for this isolated hilly site before municipal sewerage connection
- Main and Phase 1+2 combined also have STP allocated in WOSB — suggests sequential adoption of sewerage infrastructure as the township matures
- Phases served by pump station & force main instead of gravity STP are cheaper per unit

### 4. Phase 5A&5B — Extreme cost outlier

- MYR 54.12/sqft GFA vs MYR 17.50 for flat Main
- Retaining walls alone: MYR 18.26/sqft GFA — dwarfs all other phases
- This implies very steep slopes on this sub-parcel; likely boulders/rock requiring specialist slope works
- Small scale (58 units) compounds the cost

---

## BOQ Highlights

- **Phase 1 surface water:** 750mm–1,500mm dia pipe culverts + 1,500–1,800mm box culverts — large infrastructure for 106 units, driven by hill catchment
- **Phase 2 surface water:** OSD tank included; 600mm–1,050mm dia pipe — smaller scale but similar OSD requirement
- **Main surface water:** Full OSD + RC sumps + 900mm pipe + 600mm–3,000mm RC PC U-drain and cascading drains — township-scale catchment management
- **Phase 5A&5B retaining walls (WWSB MYR 18.26/sqft):** Far exceeds any other phase — significant structural slope retention required

---

## Contractor & Procurement Notes

- Report reference: Project 4236, "Based on CCE 2A" (CCE = Concept Cost Estimate, revision 2A)
- Date: 2 October 2025
- QS/author: Internal cost report (Author: Phon)
- All figures are **estimates** at CCE stage — tender/contract-award figures may vary
- No main contractor stated
- Phase 1 has self-contained STP (DOE compliance); other phases connect to sewerage network

---

---

## Building Superstructure Costs — DCS 19B vs CCE No 2 vs CCE No 2A

> Source: Project 4236 — "Raintree Forest Sg Long — Comparison of DCS 19B, CCE No 2 and CCE No 2A", dated 26 Nov 2024. All rates on **NFA (Net Floor Area)** basis. For landed houses, NFA ≈ GFA.

### Building Cost by Unit Type (MYR/sqft NFA)

| Unit Type | DCS 19B NFA (sqft) | DCS RM/sqft | CCE No 2 NFA | CCE2 RM/sqft | CCE No 2A NFA | CCE2A RM/sqft | CCE2A Total (MYR) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Semi D | 759,000 | 194.00 | 896,139 | 204.93 | 829,560 | **208.72** | 173,145,000 |
| Zero Lot | 94,500 | 196.00 | 114,278 | 193.68 | 107,828 | **199.14** | 21,473,000 |
| Villa | 66,000 | 202.00 | 68,408 | 196.85 | 64,600 | **199.61** | 12,895,000 |
| RSKU (affordable) | 216,150 | 199.01 | 216,150 | 224.45 | 216,150 | **224.45** | 48,514,000 |
| Clubhouse | 10,000 | 300.00 | 38,569 | 203.48 | 38,569 | **203.48** | 7,848,000 |
| **Total** | **1,145,650** | — | **1,333,544** | — | **1,256,707** | — | **263,875,000** |

### Total Development Cost Summary — CCE No 2A

| Component | MYR | % of Sub-Total |
|---|---|---|
| Building superstructure (all types) | 263,875,000 | 78.5% |
| Infrastructure within site | 41,374,000 | 12.3% |
| Infrastructure outside site | 4,113,000 | 1.2% |
| M&E Infrastructure | 8,593,000 | 2.6% |
| Landscape | 7,062,000 | 2.1% |
| Other provisions (fencing, CCTV, STP, detention pond, etc.) | 11,285,000 | 3.4% |
| **Sub-Total** | **336,302,000** | **100%** |
| Prelims (7.0%) | 23,541,000 | — |
| Contingency (3.0%) | 10,090,000 | — |
| **Grand Total (CCE No 2A)** | **369,933,000** | — |

> All-in development cost: **MYR 369.9M** for ~1.26M sqft residential NFA = **MYR ~294/sqft NFA blended** (building + infra + prelims + contingency, excl. land/fees/S&M/financing).

### Three-Revision Grand Total Comparison

| Revision | Grand Total (MYR) | vs DCS Base |
|---|---|---|
| DCS 19B | 316,092,561 | — (base) |
| CCE No 2 | 382,840,000 | **+21.12%** |
| CCE No 2A | 369,933,000 | **+17.03%** |

### Infrastructure Within Site — DCS vs CCE2A Breakdown

| Item | DCS (MYR) | CCE No 2A (MYR) | Change |
|---|---|---|---|
| Site Clearance & Earthworks | 1,340,265 | 4,382,000 | +227% ⚠️ soil theft |
| Retaining Walls (within unit lots) | 2,474,800 | 2,270,000 | -8% |
| Road Works | 6,195,194 | 10,021,000 | +62% |
| Surface Water Drainage | 9,227,840 | 11,670,000 | +26% |
| Foul Drainage | 4,248,400 | 4,399,000 | +4% |
| Sewerage Treatment Plant | 1,450,000 | 2,180,000 | +50% |
| Water Reticulation | 4,794,700 | 3,450,000 | -28% |
| Water Reservoir | 2,036,365 | 2,070,000 | +2% |
| Fencing & Gate | 1,880,055 | 932,000 | -50% |
| **Total within site** | **33,647,618** | **41,374,000** | **+23%** |

> ⚠️ The DCS earthworks undercount (MYR 1.34M → 4.38M) is annotated in the source as "soil stolen" — site topsoil was removed by unknown parties before the DCS was finalised. CCE2A adds MYR 1.74M for soil replacement and slope strengthening (item 12 in source).

### Key Observations

1. **Building dominates cost structure**: At 78.5% of sub-total, building superstructure dwarfs infrastructure. The infrastructure-to-building ratio of ~14:86 is typical for hilly mid-density landed (vs flat high-density where infrastructure per NFA sqft is lower).

2. **RSKU spec uplift**: RSKU rate rose from DCS MYR 199.01 to CCE2/2A MYR 224.45 (+12.8%). The NFA remained unchanged (216,150 sqft) suggesting a spec change rather than a quantity change. The driver is not confirmed in the source.

3. **Clubhouse scope expanded 3.86×**: DCS 10,000 sqft → CCE2/2A 38,569 sqft NFA. Rate fell from MYR 300 to MYR 203/sqft as fixed costs are spread over larger floor area.

4. **CCE2 → CCE2A revision reduces cost**: Grand total dropped from MYR 382.8M (CCE2) to MYR 369.9M (CCE2A), a MYR 12.9M reduction (-3.4%). Primary driver: lower Semi D NFA in CCE2A (829k vs 896k sqft) — fewer or smaller units than assumed in CCE2.

5. **Landscape rationalised downward**: DCS landscape MYR 11.52M → CCE2A MYR 7.06M (-39%). May reflect scope reduction or more realistic LA allowance replacing early DCS placeholder.

---

## Sources

- `[[raw-sources/construction cost/Infra Cost (Landed houses) 20251002.pdf]]` — infrastructure cost comparison by phase (Oct 2025, based on CCE 2A)
- `[[raw-sources/construction cost/4236. Cost Comparison of DCS 19B, CCE No 2 and CCE No 2A.pdf]]` — full development cost comparison DCS vs CCE2 vs CCE2A (Nov 2024)
- [[sources/2026-04-29-raintree-forest-cost-comparison-4236]]

## Related Pages

- [[construction-costs/rates/external-works]] — infrastructure unit rate benchmarks (hilly vs flat terrain)
- [[construction-costs/rates/landed-house-building-cost]] — building superstructure RM/sqft NFA benchmarks by unit type

notes.title: doors-windows
notes.content:
# Doors, Windows & Ironmongery — Rate Reference

## Category Overview

Covers Elements 2G (Windows) and 2H (Doors) in Malaysian QS practice:
- **2G Windows:** Aluminium-framed sliding/casement windows; sliding glass doors to balcony; louvres; fixed glazing; ironmongery
- **2H Doors:** Hollow-core or solid-core main entrance doors; bedroom doors; bathroom doors; fire-rated doors to staircases/lobbies; service doors; ironmongery

In residential high-rise, door cost per m² GFA is driven primarily by the number of units (more units = more entrance doors) and fire door requirements (one pair per floor per staircase). Windows are driven by unit size and façade glazing ratio.

---

## Rate Table

> Data from one project (BRDB Semenyih, Sep 2024 estimate). All rates are elemental cost per m² GFA.

### Windows (Element 2G)

| Item | Spec / Notes | Unit | Rate (MYR) | Date | Project |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Windows — FMH Residential Tower | Aluminium sliding windows + balcony sliding door; standard spec | /m² GFA | 41 | 2024-Q3 | [[construction-costs/projects/brdb-semenyih]] |
| Windows — RSKU Affordable Tower | Similar aluminium spec | /m² GFA | 43 | 2024-Q3 | [[construction-costs/projects/brdb-semenyih]] |
| Windows — Facilities Floor | Higher glazing ratio; feature glazing to amenity areas | /m² GFA | 51 | 2024-Q3 | [[construction-costs/projects/brdb-semenyih]] |
| Windows — Podium Carpark | Minimal — louvres / openings only; no glazing | /m² GFA | <1 | 2024-Q3 | [[construction-costs/projects/brdb-semenyih]] |

### Doors (Element 2H)

| Item | Spec / Notes | Unit | Rate (MYR) | Date | Project |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Doors — FMH Residential Tower | Main entrance door + bedroom/bathroom doors + fire doors; standard spec | /m² GFA | 67 | 2024-Q3 | [[construction-costs/projects/brdb-semenyih]] |
| Doors — RSKU Affordable Tower | Similar count but simpler ironmongery | /m² GFA | 82 | 2024-Q3 | [[construction-costs/projects/brdb-semenyih]] |
| Doors — Facilities Floor | Feature entrance doors; higher ironmongery spec | /m² GFA | 15–17 | 2024-Q3 | [[construction-costs/projects/brdb-semenyih]] |
| Doors — Podium Carpark | Fire/service doors only; car park shutters | /m² GFA | 2–4 | 2024-Q3 | [[construction-costs/projects/brdb-semenyih]] |

---

## Notes & Assumptions

- All rates from JKQS Cost Estimate Report No. 2b (JK506), September 2024. Classified as **estimate**.
- RSKU doors (MYR 82/m²) appear higher per m² GFA than FMH (MYR 67/m²) — likely because RSKU has smaller units (more doors per m² of GFA) and the cost is spread over a smaller total GFA.
- For luxury residential (e.g. KLCC corridor), solid timber/veneer entrance doors with smart lock ironmongery can push door costs to MYR 120–200/m² GFA.
- Window rate of MYR 41–43/m² GFA for standard aluminium spec is consistent with mass market Malaysian residential norms. Curtain wall or premium system windows would be captured under External Finishes (façade) rather than this element.
- Escalate from 2024 base at ~5–8% p.a.

---

## Sources

- [[construction-costs/projects/brdb-semenyih]] — JKQS Cost Estimate Report No. 2b, Sep 2024

notes.title: external-finishes
notes.content:
# External Finishes — Rate Reference

## Category Overview

Covers Elements 2E (External Walls) and 3D (External Finishes) in Malaysian QS practice:
- **2E External Walls:** Brickwork / blockwork to external faces; external façade systems (paint, texture coat, cladding, curtain wall, composite panel)
- **3D External Finishes:** External wall finishes (render, paint, tile); external floor finishes (paving, hardscape); external ceiling finishes (soffit paint/cladding at covered walkways, canopies)

Façade is one of the largest specification-driven variables in residential construction. A mass market project may use painted plaster at MYR 30–50/m² GFA while a luxury project with full curtain wall or GRC can reach MYR 150–300/m² GFA.

---

## Rate Table

> Data from one project (BRDB Semenyih, Sep 2024 estimate). All rates are elemental cost per m² GFA.

### 2E External Walls (Brickwork + Façade)

| Item | Spec / Notes | Unit | Rate (MYR) | Date | Project |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| External Walls (total) — FMH Residential Tower | Brickwork + painted/textured façade | /m² GFA | 48–49 | 2024-Q3 | [[construction-costs/projects/brdb-semenyih]] |
| — Brickwork component | Normal brickwork to external walls | /m² GFA | 26 | 2024-Q3 | [[construction-costs/projects/brdb-semenyih]] |
| — External Façade component | Texture paint / rendering / minor cladding | /m² GFA | 22 | 2024-Q3 | [[construction-costs/projects/brdb-semenyih]] |
| External Walls — RSKU Affordable Tower | Brickwork + basic paint; no cladding | /m² GFA | 29 | 2024-Q3 | [[construction-costs/projects/brdb-semenyih]] |
| External Walls — Podium Carpark | Minimal — exposed structure or basic paint | /m² GFA | 10 | 2024-Q3 | [[construction-costs/projects/brdb-semenyih]] |

### 3D External Finishes

| Item | Spec / Notes | Unit | Rate (MYR) | Date | Project |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| External Wall Finishes — FMH Tower | Paint, texture coat, minor tiling to feature areas | /m² GFA | 46–47 | 2024-Q3 | [[construction-costs/projects/brdb-semenyih]] |
| External Wall Finishes — RSKU Tower | Basic external paint | /m² GFA | 41 | 2024-Q3 | [[construction-costs/projects/brdb-semenyih]] |
| External Floor Finishes — FMH Podium | Pool deck, drop-off paving, entrance paving | /m² GFA | 14 | 2024-Q3 | [[construction-costs/projects/brdb-semenyih]] |
| External Floor Finishes — Facilities | Roof garden / deck finishes | /m² GFA | 137 | 2024-Q3 | [[construction-costs/projects/brdb-semenyih]] |
| External Ceiling Finishes | Soffit of canopy / covered linkways | /m² GFA | 2–5 | 2024-Q3 | [[construction-costs/projects/brdb-semenyih]] |

---

## Notes & Assumptions

- All rates from JKQS Cost Estimate Report No. 2b (JK506), September 2024. Classified as **estimate**.
- This project uses painted/textured plaster façade (no curtain wall). Façade cost of MYR 22/m² GFA is at the low end of the spectrum — typical mass market Selangor suburban spec.
- For comparison: aluminium curtain wall systems on luxury KL high-rise typically run MYR 80–150/m² GFA; GRC cladding MYR 60–100/m² GFA; composite panel MYR 40–70/m² GFA.
- The high external floor finishes rate for the Facilities floor (MYR 137/m² GFA) reflects rooftop amenity deck / pool deck treatment on a small floor plate — cost is high per GFA unit because the area is small relative to total GFA.
- Escalate from 2024 base at ~5–8% p.a.

---

## Sources

- [[construction-costs/projects/brdb-semenyih]] — JKQS Cost Estimate Report No. 2b, Sep 2024
