notes.title: KLCC / Bukit Bintang
notes.content:
# KLCC / Bukit Bintang

## Overview

The KLCC–Bukit Bintang Golden Triangle is KL's primary commercial, luxury residential, and tourism core. It sits within the **CCC (City Centre Commercial)** zone under KLDCP 2024 — the highest-intensity development zone in Malaysia, with base plot ratios up to **1:10** and potential uplift to 1:12 via TPZ incentives. The area is anchored by Petronas Twin Towers, KLCC Park, Pavilion KL, Bukit Bintang street, and the new Tun Razak Exchange (TRX).

Zone: CCC (City Centre Commercial) — DBKL jurisdiction
Planning reference: [[concepts/kl-development-control]], [[concepts/tpz-tiz-transit-incentives]]

---

## Residential Market

- Price range: **RM 1,000–2,500+ PSF** (among the highest in Malaysia)
- Dominated by high-rise condominiums and serviced apartments (A10 commercial class)
- Foreign buyer magnet — RM 1M threshold easily met; strong Singapore/HK/Chinese buyer interest historically
- **Vacancy concern:** Some towers have persistently high vacancy; check occupancy before assuming rental income
- Key projects: Four Seasons Place, The Ritz-Carlton Residences, Stonor Park, Binjai 8, Minh Capital Tower vicinity

---

## Commercial / Office

- TRX: Malaysia's new international financial district; The Exchange TRX (mall) + TRX Residences + Grade A offices
- KLCC: Petronas Twin Towers (offices), Suria KLCC (prime retail) — fully occupied; no investment access
- Merdeka 118: tallest building in Southeast Asia; premium office + Four Seasons Hotel
- **Office vacancy:** Elevated post-2023 with new TRX/Merdeka supply; watch for absorption timeline

---

## Key Planning Facts (from KLDCP 2024)

- **Zone:** CCC — base plot ratio up to **1:10**
- **Height:** Up to 14 storeys for commercial terrace; tall towers subject to individual planning approval
- **TPZ Category 1 stations (highest +20% bonus):**
  - MRT1: Bukit Bintang, Merdeka, Pasar Seni
  - MRT2: Persiaran KLCC, Ampang Park, TRX
  - LRT Kelana Jaya: KLCC, Ampang Park
  - Monorail: Bukit Bintang
- **TRX Development Code** applies to TRX precinct (separate design control document)
- Jalan Bukit Bintang designated as future pedestrian/micromobility street (KLDCP Vol 2)
- Jalan Ampang designated as eco-district

---

## Yield Snapshot

| Segment | Gross Yield | Notes |
|---------|------------|-------|
| Luxury condo (B1) | 2.5–4% | Capital appreciation play; low yield on high entry price |
| Serviced apartment (A10) | 3–5% | Commercial title; commercial utility tariff applies |
| Prime retail (Pavilion/KLCC) | N/A (institutional-owned) | Not accessible to retail investors |
| Grade A office | 4–5.5% | TRX new supply creating short-term vacancy pressure |

---

## Risks & Watch Points

- Serviced apartment oversupply in some towers — check NAPIC vacancy data
- Commercial title (A10) for most new high-rise launches — affects loan terms and utility costs
- Heritage zone buffer (200m from pre-war KL heritage areas) constrains some heights
- New TRX and Merdeka supply absorbing office demand; watch vacancy trend

---

## 2025 Launch PSF Data

Based on HB Research's tracking of 2025 new launches, **2 projects** were launched in the KLCC area:
- **PSF range: MYR 1,500–3,600 psf** — the widest spread in KL, spanning from high-rise serviced apartments to ultra-luxury towers

KL's overall residential transaction value surged **+26.4%** in 2025 while volume grew +8.7%, confirming that high-value city-centre transactions were the primary driver of KL's strong performance.

*Source: [[sources/2026-04-28-hb-advisor-2q26]]*

---

## KLCCP Stapled Group — FY2025 Retail & Hotel Performance

Key data from KLCCP Stapled Group (KL:KLCC) AGM, April 28, 2026:

| Metric | FY2025 | FY2024 | Change |
|--------|--------|--------|--------|
| Net profit | **RM1.28 billion** | RM1.02 billion | +25.95% |
| Revenue | RM1.74 billion | RM1.71 billion | +1.69% |
| Dividend (per stapled security) | **47 sen** | — | Record |

**Suria KLCC retail:**
- Annual tenant turnover stabilised at ~**RM2.7 billion** (peak: slightly above RM3 billion)
- Tenant occupancy costs below 20% of revenue on average — described as manageable
- Single-digit rental growth; management extracting profit via cost discipline and operational efficiency
- Record FY2025 net profit driven by cost management despite single-digit revenue growth

**Cost pressure watch (2026):**
- Rising costs flagged for retail and hotel segments amid Middle East tensions and global supply chain disruption
- Impact described as "expected to remain manageable" — group focused on cost discipline and sustainability initiatives
- Single-digit rental growth trajectory expected to continue

**Hotel:**
- Mandarin Oriental KL optimising rooms, MICE (meetings, incentives, conferences, exhibitions), lifestyle
- Newly renovated Grand Ballroom supports MICE positioning
- Capturing demand from key Asian growth markets

**Corporate strategy:** Focus firmly on domestic opportunities; no plans for overseas expansion as of April 2026.

*Source: [[sources/2026-04-29-the-edge-ceo-morning-brief-issue-1144]] — KLCCP AGM (p. 8)*

---

## Sources

- [[sources/2026-04-28-klsp2040]]
- [[sources/2026-04-28-kldcp-v1p1-development-control]]
- [[sources/2026-04-28-kldcp-v2-promoting-city-development]]
- [[sources/2026-04-28-hb-advisor-2q26]]
- [[sources/2026-04-29-the-edge-ceo-morning-brief-issue-1144]] — KLCCP FY2025 results; Knight Frank KL luxury +1.1% 2025

notes.title: Langkawi
notes.content:
# Langkawi

## Overview

Langkawi is an archipelago of 99 islands off the northwest coast of Peninsular Malaysia in Kedah state. It is Malaysia's premier resort island destination and a duty-free zone. Langkawi's property market is niche — primarily resort, tourism hospitality, and commercial assets — with limited residential liquidity compared to Klang Valley or Penang.

Development is regulated by the **Langkawi Development Authority (LADA)**, a statutory body under the federal government. LADA designates land use zones including agro-tourism and commercial categories, and its approvals are a key gating factor for development.

The island's economy is primarily driven by tourism and duty-free retail. Key infrastructure includes **Langkawi International Airport** (served by multiple airlines; gateway for tourists) and the Kuah Jetty (ferry connections).

---

## Residential Market

- Limited residential market; mostly holiday homes, resort villas, serviced apartments
- Foreign ownership allowed in certain zones (Langkawi is a special economic zone — foreigners can purchase in specific approved developments)
- Pricing data sparse; resort-linked units typically priced at RM500–1,200 PSF depending on brand, waterfront access, and concept
- Liquidity is low — secondary market is thin; long hold periods typical

---

## Commercial Market

- Duty-free retail is the primary commercial driver (Kuah, Pantai Cenang, Langkawi Mall)
- Hotel and resort assets command premium yields driven by tourism occupancy
- Commercial land in agro-tourism zones being targeted by developers for mixed-use resort-commercial concepts (Tropicana acquisition, April 2026)

---

## Industrial Market

- No significant industrial market; island is tourism-oriented
- Some light industrial near airport (logistics, cold chain for duty-free goods)

---

## Key Developments

### Tropicana Corp — 24.15-Acre Acquisition (April 2026)

Tropicana Corp Bhd (KL:TROP) acquired **15 parcels totalling 24.15 acres** in Langkawi for **RM195.88 million** via its wholly-owned subsidiary Tropicana Scenic Development Sdn Bhd (TSDSB):

**Transaction 1:**
- 14 parcels in **Bandar Padang Lalang, Langkawi**
- Vendor: Maya Elemen Sdn Bhd
- Consideration: **RM151.1 million**
- Designation: LADA-identified key **agro-tourism and commercial zone**

**Transaction 2:**
- 1 leasehold parcel in **Padang Matsirat**
- Vendor: Tanjung Mali Resort Development Sdn Bhd
- Consideration: **RM44.8 million**
- Location: Near Langkawi International Airport
- Land type: Leasehold

**Implied land cost:** RM195.88M ÷ 24.15 acres = ~RM8.1M per acre (~RM186 psf of land area). This is a significant premium for Langkawi land, reflecting the strategic LADA-designated agro-tourism/commercial zone classification and airport proximity.

No GDV or development concept has been announced by Tropicana as of April 2026.

---

## Yield Snapshot

*Limited data in this wiki. Resort hospitality yields in Langkawi typically 5–8% gross for branded hotel assets. Commercial retail yields near Kuah town 4–6% gross. Residential/holiday home yields highly variable and liquidity-dependent.*

---

## Risks & Watch Points

- **LADA regulatory risk**: All significant developments require LADA approval; government priorities can shift
- **Tourism dependency**: Property values tied to tourist arrivals and AirAsia/tourism sector health
- **Leasehold exposure**: Padang Matsirat parcel is leasehold — typical for Langkawi; tenure and renewal terms matter
- **Low liquidity**: Secondary market thin; Langkawi property is a long-hold/operational asset, not a trading market
- **Development concept risk**: Tropicana has not disclosed intended use; agro-tourism concept may have limited appeal to mainstream buyers if not properly anchored
- **Duty-free competition**: If duty-free privileges were ever reviewed (unlikely but possible), commercial retail would be severely impacted

---

## Sources

- [[sources/2026-04-29-the-edge-ceo-morning-brief-issue-1144]] — Tropicana Langkawi acquisition (p. 13)

notes.title: Mont Kiara / Sri Hartamas
notes.content:
# Mont Kiara / Sri Hartamas

## Overview

Mont Kiara and adjacent Sri Hartamas form KL's premier expatriate residential enclave, ~8 km northwest of KLCC. Known for large-format condominiums, international schools, and a strong F&B/lifestyle scene. Both areas are under **special density control (PPA — Persons Per Acre)** rather than standard plot ratio, reflecting the large-unit character DBKL wants to preserve. Critically, both are designated future **MRT3 (Circle Line) station locations**, which is the single biggest value catalyst for the area.

---

## Residential Market

- Price range: **RM 700–1,200 PSF** for established condos; newer launches RM 625–1,200 PSF (per UrbanMetry July 2025 competitor data)
- **Large unit dominance:** 1,500–3,000+ sq ft typical; caters to expat families
- New launch PSF (≥1,500 sf): **under RM 1,000 PSF** — significantly below [[entities/areas/damansara-heights|Damansara Heights]] (RM 1,400–1,700) and [[entities/areas/klcc-bukit-bintang|KLCC]] (RM 2,300)
- **PPA (Persons Per Acre) control** applies — only at these 5 KL locations: Mont Kiara, Jalan Robson, Jalan U-Thant, Bukit Pantai, Bukit Bangsar (KLDCP 2024)
- Units >1,600 sq ft are subject to PPA rather than plot ratio — limits density intensification
- Strong expat rental demand historically; yield 4–5.5% gross
- Significant expat population from Japan, Korea, Europe; proximity to international schools (Garden International School, Mont Kiara International School, Cempaka, French School)

---

## Commercial

**Retail / Malls (UrbanMetry July 2025 data):**
- **Plaza Arkadia** — 430,000 sf NLA, 90% occupancy, 928,950 monthly footfall (highest in study area), Village Grocer anchor
- **1 Mont Kiara** — 241,781 sf NLA, 75% occupancy, 619,800 monthly footfall
- **Hartamas Shopping Centre** — 200,000 sf NLA, 83% occupancy, 618,750 monthly footfall
- **Sunway 163 Mall** — 255,535 sf NLA, 95% occupancy, 675,750 monthly footfall, Jaya Grocer anchor
- **Publika** — 335,000 sf NLA, 83% occupancy
- **Solaris Mont Kiara** — boutique office + F&B strip
- Mont Kiara malls attract M40 visitors primarily; lower T10/T5 share (15–21%) vs Bukit Damansara/Bangsar malls (33–37% T5)
- Median visitor travel distance: 3–6 km (tighter local catchment than Bukit Damansara at 6–9 km)

**Strata Offices:**
- Mont Kiara strata office asking rent: ~RM 5.00 PSF (same as downtown KL; below Pavilion D'Sara Heights at RM 7.50)
- Incoming: Solaris Parq The Office Strata Offices (2029)

**Key Competitors (Incoming Residential — July 2025):**
- [[entities/developers/uem-sunrise|The Minh @ MK]] (UEM Sunrise) — Tower A: 245 units, 87% sold; Tower B: 251 units, 90% sold; RM 809 PSF
- [[entities/developers/kerjaya-prospek|Papyrus North Kiara]] (Kerjaya Prospek) — 454 units, RM 675 PSF, 46% SPA / 85% reported
- [[entities/developers/asia-quest-holdings|Kiaramas Dedaun]] (Asia Quest) — Phase 1: 436 units, 68% sold; Phase 2: 223 units, 31% sold; RM 784–797 PSF
- [[entities/developers/wct-land|Pavilion Mont Kiara]] (WCT Land) — 336 units, RM 1,199 PSF, 99% sold
- [[entities/developers/brdb|Miranda Hill]] (BRDB) — 552 units, RM 697 PSF, 89% sold
- [[entities/developers/bon-estates|Bon Kiara]] (Bon Estates) — 410 units, RM 625 PSF, 90% sold — lowest PSF, strong take-up

---

## Key Planning Facts (from KLDCP 2024)

- **Large unit residential enclave** designation maintained — PPA control (not plot ratio)
- **MRT3 (Circle Line — proposed):** Stations at both Mont Kiara AND Sri Hartamas — if built, transforms connectivity from car-dependent to transit-linked
  - Current status: MRT3 funding and timeline uncertain as of 2026 — **critical watchpoint**
- No current MRT/LRT access — entirely car-dependent today; major limitation on rental yield and liquidity

---

## Yield Snapshot

| Segment | Gross Yield | Notes |
|---------|------------|-------|
| Expat condo (large unit) | 4–5.5% | Driven by strong expat rental demand |
| Smaller units (<1,000 sqft) | 4.5–6% | Less common; higher yield on lower entry |
| Retail / F&B | 4–6% | Good occupancy in established strips |

---

## Risks & Watch Points

- **MRT3 delay risk** — entire transit connectivity thesis depends on Circle Line being funded and built
- Car-dependent today — traffic on Jalan Duta / Sprint Highway is severe at peak hours
- Expat population sensitive to MNC relocation decisions (if MNCs pull back from KL, rental demand softens)
- Ageing condo stock in older projects — maintenance levy increases and deferred capex risk
- Maintenance fees among the highest in KL for large-format luxury condos

---

## 2025 Launch PSF Data

**1 project** launched in Mont Kiara in 2025 (highrise): **MYR 800–850 psf**. This is at the lower end of Mont Kiara's historical PSF range — consistent with the market stabilising after several years of PPA controls and expat rental demand normalisation.

*Source: [[sources/2026-04-28-hb-advisor-2q26]]*

---

## Sources

- [[sources/2026-04-28-klsp2040]]
- [[sources/2026-04-28-kldcp-v1p1-development-control]]
- [[sources/2026-04-28-kldcp-v2-promoting-city-development]]
- [[sources/2025-07-17-urbanmetry-damansara-heights-residential]]
- [[sources/2026-04-28-hb-advisor-2q26]]
- [[sources/2025-07-17-urbanmetry-damansara-heights-retail-offices]]

notes.title: Penang
notes.content:
# Penang

## Overview

Penang is Malaysia's most constrained property market by land area on the island side, which structurally supports prices. George Town (UNESCO World Heritage) adds a layer of heritage regulation that limits development in the inner city. The island is the premium market; Seberang Perai (mainland) is the growth and affordable market. Penang is also a significant manufacturing hub — it hosts a large share of Malaysia's semiconductor/electronics industry, driving industrial demand in Batu Kawan and Prai.

**Key submarkets (Island):** George Town / KOMTAR (heritage/commercial), Gurney Drive (luxury condo), Batu Ferringhi (resort/tourism), Tanjung Tokong / Tanjung Bungah (mid-high residential), Jelutong / Gelugor (mid-market), Bayan Lepas (industrial + affordable residential near airport).

**Key submarkets (Mainland):** Butterworth/Seberang Jaya (older commercial), Bukit Mertajam, Batu Kawan (new township + industrial), Simpang Ampat.

---

## Residential Market

- **Gurney Drive / Gurney Paragon area:** RM 700–1,300 PSF; premium condos; strong rental from expats and professionals
- **Tanjung Tokong / Tanjung Bungah:** RM 500–900 PSF; popular with locals upgrading; seaview premium
- **Batu Ferringhi:** RM 400–700 PSF; resort market; seasonal demand, lower year-round yield
- **Bayan Lepas / Bayan Baru:** RM 350–550 PSF; near airport; mid-market; electronics workers
- **Mainland (Batu Kawan, Simpang Ampat):** RM 200–400 PSF; fast-growing township; Ikea / Design Village anchor
- **Mainland (Butterworth/Seberang Jaya):** RM 200–350 PSF; mature; affordable; lower capital appreciation outlook

**Key dynamics:** Very limited new freehold land on island — most new launches on reclaimed land or leasehold. Penang reclamation projects (Penang South Reclamation / PSR) — monitor approval and launch status.

---

## Commercial Market

- George Town shophouses: RM 500–1,500 PSF; heritage shophouses attract boutique hotel / F&B / co-working use; limited supply, strong demand
- Gurney Plaza / Gurney Paragon area: prime retail
- Office: limited Grade A supply; most office demand absorbed into shophouses or converted shopoffices

---

## Industrial Market

- **Bayan Lepas Free Industrial Zone (FIZ):** anchor for electronics/semiconductor; tight; established multinationals
- **Penang Science Park / Batu Maung:** high-tech industrial; R&D facilities
- **Prai Industrial Estate:** Seberang Perai; chemical/heavy manufacturing
- **Batu Kawan Industrial Park:** newer; attracting semiconductor suppliers, logistics; competitive with Johor for some FDI
- **Yields:** 5–7%; land values rising with FDI demand

---

## Key Developments / Projects

*To be populated as sources are ingested.*

---

## Yield Snapshot

| Asset Class | Gross Yield Range | Notes |
|-------------|-------------------|-------|
| Residential (island condo) | 3.5–5% | Expat rental belt (Tanjung Tokong) pushes yield up |
| Residential (mainland) | 4–6% | Higher yield, lower capital appreciation base |
| Heritage shophouse | 3–5% | Capital appreciation strong; yield lower due to high capital values |
| Industrial | 5–7% | Strong FDI-driven demand |

---

## Risks & Watch Points

- Heritage zone restrictions — George Town inner city approvals slow
- Reclamation projects face environmental and political opposition
- Penang island prices may have outpaced fundamentals in some segments
- Mainland connectivity (Penang Second Bridge, upcoming LRT) key for mainland growth
- Semiconductor industry concentration risk — global downturn hits industrial demand

---

## 2025 Market Data (NAPIC)

Penang had a weak 2025 — one of only a few states declining in both volume and value across multiple sub-sectors:

| Sub-sector | Volume Change | Value Change |
|---|---|---|
| Overall | -3.9% | **-5.0%** |
| Residential | -4.9% | **-7.0%** |
| Commercial | -2.0% | **-24.9%** |
| Industrial | -2.0% | +5.2% |

Penang's residential and commercial declines suggest a market correction after strong prior years. The -24.9% commercial value decline is particularly sharp — likely driven by a few fewer large commercial transactions or a softening in service apartment pricing. Penang was also in the top 4 for residential unsold under-construction stock (10.8% of national total). Industrial held up due to ongoing semiconductor/electronics FDI demand.

*Source: [[sources/2026-04-28-hb-advisor-2q26]]*

---

## Sources

- [[sources/2026-04-28-hb-advisor-2q26]]

notes.title: Sentul
notes.content:
# Sentul

## Overview

Sentul is a regeneration suburb ~3 km north of KLCC, historically a working-class and railway workers' district centred around KTM's former Sentul Workshop complex. The area has undergone significant redevelopment since the 2000s (YTL Land's Sentul West and Sentul East townships transformed the KTM land). Now designated a **Secondary Growth Centre** under KLDCP 2024, with emphasis on high-value employment and affordable housing.

---

## Residential Market

- Price range: RM 350–650 PSF for mid-market condos; rising with regeneration
- Historical entry point much lower than adjacent Chow Kit / Jalan Ipoh corridor
- YTL's Sentul West (The Capers, The Fennel, The Sage) set the premium tier
- Sentul East: more affordable; closer to light industrial transition
- Good connectivity: MRT2 Sentul Barat station + KTM Sentul station — dual transit

---

## Commercial / Employment

- KLDCP Vol 2 target: focus on **high-value employment** zone
- Existing anchor: Dewan Filharmonik Petronas (original arts centre by YTL); boutique F&B cluster
- Transition from light industrial to mixed commercial underway

---

## Key Planning Facts (from KLDCP 2024)

- **Secondary Growth Centre** (Vol 2)
- **Affordable housing emphasis** in all redevelopment — Control Zone 2 (MRT3 corridor)
- MRT2 Sentul Barat station: **TPZ Category** (exact category — verify from DBKL station list)
- KTM Sentul: existing commuter rail connection to KL Sentral
- Pedestrian connectivity routes from Sentul West/East to both stations targeted for improvement

---

## Yield Snapshot

| Segment | Gross Yield | Notes |
|---------|------------|-------|
| Mid-market condo | 4.5–6.5% | Affordable entry price; rental demand from KL workers |
| YTL premium tier | 3.5–5% | Higher entry price; smaller yield |

---

## Risks & Watch Points

- Some blocks face maintenance issues in older non-YTL stock
- Industrial legacy — some surrounding pockets still light industrial; affects liveability perception
- Affordable housing obligation in redevelopment may cap developer margins

---

## 2025 Launch PSF Data

**2 projects** launched in Sentul in 2025 (highrise): **MYR 600–700 psf**. Sentul's regeneration story continues — PSF has risen considerably from its previous sub-RM500 era, reflecting MRT2 Sentul Barat connectivity and DBKL's Secondary Growth Centre designation.

*Source: [[sources/2026-04-28-hb-advisor-2q26]]*

---

## Sources

- [[sources/2026-04-28-klsp2040]]
- [[sources/2026-04-28-kldcp-v2-promoting-city-development]]
- [[sources/2026-04-28-hb-advisor-2q26]]

notes.title: Sepang / KLIA Aeropolis / IDRISS
notes.content:
# Sepang / KLIA Aeropolis / IDRISS

## Overview

Sepang is a district in southern Selangor, most prominently known as the home of **Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA)**. For property purposes, the key investment theme here is the **KLIA Aeropolis** concept — the government's vision to develop a major aviation, logistics, and industrial cluster around KLIA as the anchor.

The area sits within the broader **Integrated Development Region in South Selangor (IDRISS)**, which is the state-level economic planning framework for this corridor. The **Sepang Local Plan 2035** governs land use, with the **Sepang Infinity Corridor Hub** as the conceptual framework for integrated industrial-commercial development.

Sepang has historically been dominated by plantation land, the Formula 1 circuit (now defunct as F1 venue), and low-density development. It is now being repositioned as a strategic industrial and logistics hub, capitalising on its KLIA adjacency.

---

## Industrial Market

### SD Guthrie + MBI Selangor — 2,500-Acre Industrial Park

The most significant development announced as of April 2026:

- **SD Guthrie Bhd (KL:SDG)** and **Menteri Besar Selangor (Incorporation) (MBI Selangor)** are exploring a strategic partnership for a **2,500-acre mixed-use industrial development** in Sepang Estate
- Positioned adjacent to KLIA Aeropolis, within IDRISS
- **Target: 32,000+ job opportunities by 2030**
- Development will feature industrial infrastructure including:
  - Manufacturing and logistics facilities
  - Aerospace-related capabilities
  - Business and training centres
- Described as intended to "deliver integrated aerospace capabilities supported by sustainable and smart infrastructure"
- This is the second SD Guthrie + MBI Selangor collaboration (first: Carey Island integrated development)
- The project is envisioned as a "strategic growth node within IDRISS, strengthening the state's aviation and industrial ecosystem while capitalising on its proximity to KLIA"

**Status**: Exploring partnership (as of April 2026) — no formal JV or development agreement announced yet; MOU-stage

### Broader Industrial Positioning

- Sepang benefits from existing KLIA infrastructure — Cargo terminal, MRO (Maintenance, Repair & Overhaul) facilities, Express Rail Link (ERL) connectivity
- Aerospace and aviation services are the primary industrial anchor
- Logistics and e-commerce fulfilment increasingly active given airport adjacency
- Selangor government has been actively developing IDRISS as an industrial and investment hub to attract FDI

---

## Residential Market

Sepang's residential market is limited and mostly serves airport workers and adjacent township developments. Not a primary residential investment market. Key nearby residential areas include Nilai (Negeri Sembilan border), Sepang Gold Coast (resort), and some landed housing in Salak Tinggi.

---

## Commercial Market

Commercial development is nascent — tied primarily to airport-related services, hotels, and F&B near KLIA. The Sepang Infinity Corridor Hub is expected to generate commercial amenities as the industrial park develops, but the primary use case is industrial/logistics.

---

## Key Developments

| Development | Developer | Type | Size | Status |
|-------------|-----------|------|------|--------|
| Sepang Industrial Park (Sepang Infinity Corridor Hub) | SD Guthrie + MBI Selangor | Mixed-use industrial | 2,500 acres | Exploring partnership (2026) |
| Carey Island Integrated Development | SD Guthrie + MBI Selangor | Mixed-use | — | Earlier collaboration; completed/ongoing |

---

## Yield Snapshot

*No meaningful yield data yet — area is pre-development for the major industrial park announcement. Industrial yields in comparable Selangor parks (Shah Alam, Subang, Klang) range 5–7% gross for completed facilities.*

---

## Risks & Watch Points

- **Partnership risk**: SD Guthrie + MBI Selangor deal is at exploration stage; no binding agreement means the 2,500-acre development could be delayed or restructured
- **Infrastructure lead time**: Aerospace-grade industrial parks require significant civil, utilities, and transport infrastructure investment — 5–10 year development horizon realistic
- **Land title/conversion**: MBI Selangor likely holds state land; conversion from plantation/agricultural to industrial requires state executive committee approval
- **Competition**: Senai Airport City (Johor), Kulim Hi-Tech Park (Kedah), and KL Aerocity (KLIA area) are competing for similar aerospace and logistics tenants
- **Dependency on aviation recovery**: Aeropolis concept linked to KLIA's growth trajectory; disruptions to air freight or MRO demand would affect tenant demand

---

## Sources

- [[sources/2026-04-29-the-edge-ceo-morning-brief-issue-1144]] — SD Guthrie + MBI Selangor announcement (p. 5)
- [[concepts/data-centre-real-estate]] — data centre real estate framework (potential future tenant class for Sepang park)
- [[market-analysis/2026-04-29-malaysia-data-centre-national-overview]] — national DC market context

notes.title: U-Thant / Embassy Row
notes.content:
# U-Thant / Embassy Row

## Overview

The U-Thant / Embassy Row corridor is KL's premier low-density luxury residential enclave, centred on Jalan U-Thant, Jalan Ampang Hilir, and the surrounding streets of the embassy district (Seksyen 89 and adjacent sections). Unlike the high-rise-dominated KLCC/Golden Triangle, this submarket is characterised by freehold bungalow land, low-rise boutique condominiums, and established green canopy. The area draws its cachet from diplomatic missions, exclusive golf (Royal Selangor Golf Club / Langgak Golf), and proximity to Jalan Ampang without the density of the city core.

Key access: Jalan Ampang corridor (LRT Ampang Park nearby); DUKE expressway. Sayfol International School on Jalan Ampang Hilir creates peak-hour traffic congestion — a consistent watch point.

---

## Residential Market

- **Price range**: MYR 1,200–1,500 PSF (freehold condominiums), with ultra-premium units targeting above MYR 1,500 PSF
- **Demand profile**: owner-occupiers, expats, landed-home downsizers, HNW buyers; not a mass-market investor play
- **Unit size preference**: Strong demand evident at 1,400–3,000 sf (The Ashwood 100% sold) and 931–1,632 sf (Impression U-Thant 95% sold); smaller high-rise (521–976 sf) also absorbs at ~95% (Skyline Embassy) but targets a different segment
- **Tenure**: Predominantly freehold — this is a core selling point in the corridor
- **Density**: Market bifurcated between low-rise boutique (10–50 units, 8–10 storeys) and large-scale high-rise towers (1,000+ units, 50–65 storeys); the boutique segment commands premium PSF

### Active / Ongoing Projects (as at early 2026)

| Project | Developer | Units | Sizes (SF) | PSF (MYR) | Take-Up | Completion | Storeys | Maint (PSF) |
|---------|-----------|-------|-----------|-----------|---------|------------|---------|-------------|
| Impression U-Thant | Yong Tai | 108 | 931–1,632 | 1,300–1,500 | 95% | Dec 2025 | 10 (1 blk) | 0.77 |
| Skyline Embassy | TS Law Land | 1,296 | 521–976 | 1,350 | 95% | 2027 | 65 (3 blk) | 0.50 |
| The Ashwood | Paramount | 354 | 1,400–3,000 | 1,200–1,300 | 100% | 2029 | 49 (2 blk) | 0.39 |

### Pipeline Projects

| Project | Developer | Units | Sizes (SF) | Target PSF | Status |
|---------|-----------|-------|-----------|-----------|--------|
| Tmn U-Thant (TBC) | [[entities/developers/avaland\|Avaland]] | TBC | TBC | TBC | Acquisition Q1 2027; GDV RM700M; land at RM1,050 psf (~81,952 sf) |
| U-Thant (Langgak Golf) | [[entities/developers/brdb\|BRDB]] | 85 | 2,080–5,000 | ~1,500 | Pre-launch (brief dated Apr 2026) |

### Other Projects in Corridor (Completed or Earlier-Stage)
The Atrium (Paramount), Residensi R8 (Revival Capital), Pavilion Embassy (Pavilion), 18 Madge (KSL), At 6 Residence (LQS), U-Thant Place (YTL), Rimbun Embassy Row (Amphil), Dedaun (Sime Darby), Noble Crest (Yong Tai), Madge Mansions (Gamuda), Sastra U-Thant (CapitaLand), Seri Ampang Hilir (Tan & Tan)

---

## Commercial Market

Minimal commercial presence within the enclave itself. The corridor connects to commercial nodes at Jalan Ampang (Intermark, Linc, retail strip) and via Jalan Tun Razak to KLCC. Skyline Embassy's location "beside the previous British High Comm land" leverages LRT connectivity and retail proximity of Linc and Intermark — explaining its high-density, smaller-unit positioning.

---

## Key Developments

- **Avaland U-Thant (TBC name)**: 3 adjoining freehold parcels, 7,612 sqm (~81,950 sf), acquired for RM86.04M (~RM1,050 psf land), zoned residential. Proposed GDV RM700M — implies ~12.3% land cost/GDV ratio. Acquisition targeted Q1 2027. Seller: Tong Ah Company Sdn Bhd. Funded via internal funds + borrowings. High-rise concept planned.
- **BRDB U-Thant (Langgak Golf)**: 85 units, ~MYR 1,500 PSF, low-rise, golf frontage — see [[entities/developers/brdb]]
- **The Ashwood** (Paramount): 354 units, 1,400–3,000 sf, MYR 1,200–1,300 PSF — 100% sold, completing 2029
- **Skyline Embassy** (TS Law Land): 1,296 units at MYR 1,350 PSF — high-rise mass market at the Ampang/Tun Razak junction end of the corridor

---

## Yield Snapshot

| Segment | Gross Yield | Notes |
|---------|------------|-------|
| Low-rise boutique condo (2,000+ sf) | 2.5–3.5% | Owner-occupier dominated; thin rental market at high unit sizes |
| Mid-rise condo (1,000–1,600 sf) | 3–4% | Better rental liquidity; expat tenant pool |
| High-rise compact (500–900 sf) | 3.5–5% | More comparable to KLCC serviced apartment yields |

*Note: No specific rental data from this source — yields estimated from corridor positioning and PSF context. Update when rental transaction data is available.*

---

## Risks & Watch Points

- **Sayfol International School traffic**: Peak-hour congestion on Jalan Ampang Hilir is noted as a persistent friction point by multiple project teams
- **Impression U-Thant construction**: Site described as using various China contractors; proximity to the BRDB site could affect buyer sentiment during construction overlap
- **High-rise supply**: Skyline Embassy (1,296 units) completing 2027 adds significant rental supply to the high-rise/compact segment; less impact on boutique low-rise segment
- **PSF sensitivity**: At MYR 1,500 PSF and unit sizes of 2,080–5,000 sf, the BRDB project targets a thin, high-value buyer pool — execution risk if macro conditions soften
- **Planning/DA status**: U-Thant (Langgak Golf) planning approval status not confirmed in available sources

---

## Sources

- [[sources/2026-04-28-brdb-u-thant-marketing-brief]]
- [[sources/2026-04-29-avaland-u-thant-land-acquisition]]
- [[sources/2026-04-29-the-edge-ceo-morning-brief-issue-1144]] — Avaland Taman U-Thant acquisition confirmed in Bursa filing (p. 13); Knight Frank KL luxury +1.1% in 2025

notes.title: BRDB Developments Sdn Bhd
notes.content:
## Overview

BRDB (Bandar Raya Developments Berhad) is a property developer with a long track record in the Klang Valley, previously listed on Bursa before being privatised. Known for quality residential developments in established KL neighbourhoods. Operates through subsidiary Freedom Estates Sdn Bhd. Founded in 1964 as Bandar Raya Developments Bhd — one of Malaysia's earliest real estate pioneers. Legacy projects include Bangsar Shopping Centre (BSC). Positioning has shifted toward concept-led, low-density luxury in mature KL enclaves.

## Active Projects

- **Miranda Hill @ North Kiara** ([[entities/areas/mont-kiara|North Kiara]]) — 552 units across 2 blocks (38 storeys), 8 units/floor, freehold/residential, 943–2,200 sf, nett from RM 657k (RM 697 PSF), density 70 units/acre on 7.84 acres. Maintenance RM 0.28 PSF (competitive). 89% SPA signed / 90% reported as of July 2025 — strong sales. Rebates: 7%, free SPA/loan legal fees/stamp duty/disbursement. Launched Q1 2023, completing 2027.

- **U-Thant (Langgak Golf)** ([[entities/areas/u-thant-embassy-row|U-Thant / Embassy Row]]) — 85-unit, low-density, low-rise freehold residential on 1.54 acres at Seksyen 89, Jalan U-Thant, KL. Plot ratio 3.84. Unit sizes 2,080–5,000 sf. Target pricing ~MYR 1,500 PSF (MYR 3.12M–6.40M per unit). Concept: "Rhythm-Inspired Living" — golf course landscape, Mid-Century Modern architecture, GEO-certified sustainability. Fit-out: Gaggenau/Bosch appliances, Hansgrohe, Villeroy & Boch, ducted VRF A/C, herringbone timber floors, Low-E glass. Target buyers: owner-occupiers, expats, landed downsizers, design-conscious minimalists. Launch timeline not confirmed as of Apr 2026 brief.

- **Semenyih Mixed Development (JK506)** (Semenyih, Selangor) — Mixed residential: Parcel 1 FMH (free market, 2 towers + podium, GFA 136,648 m²) + Parcel 2 RSKU (Rumah Selangorku affordable, 1 tower + podium, GFA 40,362 m²). Total construction cost estimate: MYR 248.95M (MYR 119.74 PSF CFA basis, Sep 2024 estimate by JKQS). Suburban Klang Valley, ~40 km south of KL. RSKU component is state-mandated affordable housing. See [[construction-costs/projects/brdb-semenyih]] for full cost breakdown.

- **Raintree Forest @ Sungai Long** (Sungai Long, Kajang, Selangor) — Landed housing township, ~30 km south-east of KL in Hulu Langat district. Mix of Semi D, Zero Lot, Villa, and RSKU affordable units across multiple phases on hilly terrain. Total estimated development cost MYR 369.9M (CCE No 2A, Nov 2024) covering ~1.26M sqft residential NFA. Building cost: Semi D MYR 209/sqft NFA, Zero Lot MYR 199/sqft, Villa MYR 200/sqft, RSKU MYR 224/sqft. All-in ~MYR 294/sqft NFA blended (building + infra + prelims + contingency, excl. land/fees). See [[construction-costs/projects/raintree-forest-sg-long]] for full cost breakdown.

## Completed Track Record

- **Bangsar Shopping Centre (BSC)** — legacy commercial landmark in Bangsar; community anchor.

## Reputation Notes

- Concept-led approach: "design vision is concept-led, not marketing-led"
- Strong premium positioning — quiet confidence, architectural quality
- Miranda Hill has achieved ~90% sales, demonstrating execution credibility

## Sources

- [[sources/2025-07-17-urbanmetry-damansara-heights-residential]]
- [[sources/2026-04-28-brdb-u-thant-marketing-brief]]
- [[construction-costs/projects/brdb-semenyih]]
- [[construction-costs/projects/raintree-forest-sg-long]]

## Area Pages

- [[entities/areas/u-thant-embassy-row]] — U-Thant (Langgak Golf)

notes.title: SD Guthrie Bhd
notes.content:
# SD Guthrie Bhd

## Overview

SD Guthrie Bhd (Bursa: SDG) is the rebranded entity of Sime Darby Plantation — one of Malaysia's largest plantation conglomerates — after its corporate restructuring. The group holds significant landbank across Malaysia, primarily from its plantation heritage, and is now actively pursuing strategic land development partnerships to unlock value from prime landholdings.

SD Guthrie is distinct from **Sime Darby Property Bhd (SDPROP)** — the residential/commercial developer arm. SD Guthrie's property plays are primarily industrial and strategic land monetisation, often in partnership with government-linked entities.

The group has established a working relationship with **MBI Selangor (Menteri Besar Selangor Incorporation)** — the Selangor state investment arm — as a key development partner for unlocking large-scale strategic parcels in Selangor.

---

## Active Projects

| Project | Location | Type | Scale | Status |
|---------|----------|------|-------|--------|
| Sepang Industrial Park (Sepang Infinity Corridor Hub) | Sepang, Selangor | Mixed-use industrial | 2,500 acres | Exploring partnership with MBI Selangor (Apr 2026) |
| Carey Island Integrated Development | Carey Island, Selangor | Mixed-use | — | Earlier collaboration; ongoing |

### Sepang Industrial Park (Flagship)

- **2,500 acres** mixed-use industrial development in Sepang Estate
- Adjacent to KLIA Aeropolis; within IDRISS (Integrated Development Region in South Selangor)
- Located within Sepang Infinity Corridor Hub under **Sepang Local Plan 2035**
- Target: **32,000+ jobs by 2030**
- Focus sectors: manufacturing, logistics, aerospace, business/training centres
- SDG chairman: Tan Sri Nik Norzrul Thani Nik Hassan Thani
- *"We are aiming to create long-term value through strategic land development and industrial clustering to support Selangor's economic transformation"*
- Partnership status: exploration/MOU stage as of April 2026; no binding JV announced

---

## Completed Track Record

- Carey Island Integrated Development (with MBI Selangor) — prior collaboration; details not yet in wiki
- Plantation estate development across Malaysia — multiple township and industrial park conversions historically

---

## Financial Health

SD Guthrie is a large-cap listed conglomerate. Financial details (revenue, net profit, gearing) not yet populated in this wiki. Refer to Bursa Malaysia filings and annual reports for current financial position.

Key structural advantage: **substantial plantation landbank** at low book value, providing option value for industrial/commercial conversion in strategic locations.

---

## Reputation Notes

- Strong government-linked credentials — historically part of the Sime Darby group, connected to PNB (Permodalan Nasional Bhd)
- MBI Selangor partnership indicates state-level endorsement and land access for development
- Track record in large-scale land development (Carey Island precedent)
- Primary risk is execution speed — large industrial parks have long gestation periods

---

## Sources

- [[sources/2026-04-29-the-edge-ceo-morning-brief-issue-1144]] — Sepang 2,500-acre industrial park announcement (p. 5)

## Area Pages

- [[entities/areas/sepang]] — primary development area

notes.title: Sime Darby Property
notes.content:
# Sime Darby Property

## Overview

Sime Darby Property is one of Malaysia's largest property developers by landbank, with ~20,000+ acres of developable land. It is a subsidiary of Sime Darby Berhad. Known for large integrated townships, strong landbank in KV (KL, Shah Alam, Nilai), and expanding into Johor. Also has industrial land development exposure. Generally Tier-1 build quality; track record on delivery is mixed on mega projects.

**Key townships:** Ara Damansara, Bukit Jelutong, SJCC, Putra Heights, Nilai Impian, Serenia City, KL East, Elmina, City of Elmina (the flagship township in Shah Alam/Kuala Selangor corridor).

---

## Active Projects

- **Jendela Residences @ Alya (KLGCC)** — 520 units, 1 block (41 storeys), 8 units/floor, leasehold/commercial (service apartment), 1,324–2,863 sf, KPKT price RM 1.26M, nett from RM 1.25M (RM 947 PSF), density 128 units/acre on 4.06 acres. Maintenance RM 0.46 PSF. **98% SPA signed** as of July 2025 — effectively sold out. Rebates: 5%, free SPA. Developer: Sime Darby KLGCC Development Sdn Bhd. Launched Q2 2021, completing 2026.
- **The Ophera @ KLGCC** — 150 units, 1 block (21 storeys), 4 units/floor, leasehold/residential, 3,079–7,523 sf (ultra-large), KPKT price RM 5.17M, nett from RM 4.4M (RM 1,429 PSF), density 26 units/acre on 5.67 acres. Maintenance RM 0.80 PSF (highest in competitor set). **47% SPA signed / 66% reported** as of July 2025. Rebates: 2%, free SPA. Developer: Sime Darby Property (Golfhome) Sdn Bhd. Launched Q3 2024, completing 2028. Ultra-luxury product targeting T1 buyers adjacent to KLGCC.

---

## Completed Track Record

- City of Elmina: large-scale integrated township; strong take-up for landed homes
- Bukit Jelutong: mature township; well-regarded for livability
- Ara Damansara: mixed; older stock; some commercial

---

## Financial Health

*Refer to latest Bursa filings and analyst reports. Populate from ingested sources.*

---

## Reputation Notes

- Build quality generally good for landed; some feedback on high-rise finishing
- Sime Darby brand carries premium; Bumiputera quota releases well-managed
- Industrial land development (e.g. Bukit Raja Industrial Park) is a growing focus

---

notes.title: Tropicana Corporation Berhad
notes.content:
# Tropicana Corporation Berhad

## Overview

Tropicana Corporation Berhad (Bursa: TROP) is a Malaysian property developer with landbank spread across the Klang Valley and selected growth areas outside KV. The group develops integrated townships, residential high-rises, and resort/commercial assets.

Tropicana has been pursuing landbank expansion in growth areas, as evidenced by its April 2026 acquisition in Langkawi — its first known foray into the Langkawi tourism market.

---

## Active Projects

| Project | Location | Type | Scale | Status |
|---------|----------|------|-------|--------|
| Langkawi Land Acquisition | Padang Lalang & Padang Matsirat, Langkawi, Kedah | Agro-tourism / commercial (TBC) | 24.15 acres | Land acquired Apr 2026; development concept TBC |

### Langkawi Acquisition (April 2026)

Tropicana acquired **15 parcels totalling 24.15 acres** in Langkawi via its wholly-owned subsidiary Tropicana Scenic Development Sdn Bhd (TSDSB) for a total of **RM195.88 million**:

**Parcel 1:** 14 parcels, Bandar Padang Lalang, Langkawi
- Vendor: Maya Elemen Sdn Bhd
- Price: **RM151.1 million**
- LADA-designated key agro-tourism and commercial zone

**Parcel 2:** Leasehold, Padang Matsirat
- Vendor: Tanjung Mali Resort Development Sdn Bhd
- Price: **RM44.8 million**
- Near Langkawi International Airport

Implied land cost: ~RM186 psf of land area (~RM8.1M per acre). No GDV or development programme announced as of acquisition date.

---

## Completed Track Record

*Not yet populated in this wiki. Tropicana has an established track record in Klang Valley township development.*

---

## Financial Health

*Not yet populated. Refer to Bursa filings (KL:TROP) for current financial position.*

---

## Reputation Notes

- Active landbank accumulation strategy in 2025–2026; seeking exposure beyond Klang Valley
- Langkawi acquisition implies a resort or tourism-anchored development play
- LADA approval process will be a key gating factor for development timeline

---

## Sources

notes.title: UEM Sunrise Berhad
notes.content:
## Overview

UEM Sunrise Berhad is a major Malaysian property developer and a subsidiary of UEM Group (linked to Khazanah Nasional). The company is active in the Klang Valley and Johor (Iskandar Malaysia), developing mixed-use, residential, and commercial projects across multiple price segments.

UEM Sunrise holds a **substantial undeveloped Johor landbank** which is seen by market analysts as significantly undervalued relative to the stock's trading price — a key driver of recurring takeover/privatisation speculation.

## Active Projects

- **The Minh @ Mont Kiara — Tower A** ([[entities/areas/mont-kiara|Mont Kiara]]) — 245 units, 42 storeys, 6 units/floor, freehold/residential, 1,607–3,010 sf, nett from RM 1.3M (RM 809 PSF), density 80 units/acre on 6.20 acres. Maintenance RM 0.35 PSF. 87% SPA signed as of July 2025. Rebates: 7%, free SPA/loan legal fees/stamp duty. Developer: Laser Tower Sdn Bhd (subsidiary).
- **The Minh @ Mont Kiara — Tower B** — 251 units, 48 storeys, same specifications as Tower A. 90% SPA signed. Launched Q4 2022, completing 2027.

## Financial Health

*Bursa filings not yet ingested. Key market indicators as of April 2026:*

| Metric | Value | Date |
|--------|-------|------|
| Share price | 66.5 sen | April 28, 2026 |
| Book value per share | **RM1.36** | April 2026 (estimated) |
| Market capitalisation | **RM3.37 billion** | April 28, 2026 |
| Price-to-book | ~0.49× | (trading at ~51% discount to book) |

The ~51% discount to book value is the core of the undervaluation argument, driven primarily by the Johor landbank carried at low historical cost.

## Johor Landbank — Corporate Action Watch

As of April 22–28, 2026, UEM Sunrise shares rallied **+32%** (adding >RM809M in market capitalisation), driven by:
- Speculation over potential **corporate takeover or privatisation**
- Analyst commentary on undervalued Johor landbank
- 103.63 million shares traded on April 28 alone

Malacca Securities analyst Loui Low: *"Buying interest in UEM Sunrise has centred on speculation that the stock could be a corporate target, citing its undervalued landbank in Johor. 'When there is a steep discount, the market will speculate on where it should move — at least towards book value per share of RM1.36.'"*

No official corporate action was announced as of April 28, 2026.

## Area Pages

- [[entities/areas/mont-kiara]] — The Minh @ Mont Kiara
- [[entities/areas/johor]] — Johor landbank

## Sources

- [[sources/2025-07-17-urbanmetry-damansara-heights-residential]]
- [[sources/2026-04-29-the-edge-ceo-morning-brief-issue-1144]] — Johor landbank / takeover speculation (p. 13)

notes.title: bon-kiara
notes.content:
# Bon Kiara — Bon Estates

## Overview

A freehold residential project by Bon Estates in Mont Kiara offering large-format units (2,081–6,226 sf) at the lowest PSF in the 12-project cohort: MYR 625 PSF. Despite the lowest price per square foot, Bon Kiara has achieved 90% SPA signed — the third-best absorption in the cohort (after Jendela 98% and Pavilion Mont Kiara 99%).

This is a striking result: large units at the lowest PSF has outperformed many higher-PSF, smaller-unit projects. Bon Estates is a niche developer active specifically in the Mont Kiara corridor.

---

## Unit Mix

| Size Range (SF) | Nett Start PSF (MYR) | Notes |
|-----------------|----------------------|-------|
| 2,081–6,226 | ~625 | Large to ultra-large; freehold Mont Kiara; value positioning |

*Detailed unit type breakdown not available in current sources.*

---

## Sales Status

- **Take-up: 90% SPA signed** (UrbanMetry data, as at mid-2025)
- 410 units; approx. 369 units sold
- 90% at MYR 625 PSF with large units starting at MYR 1.3M — exceptional absorption by value positioning

---

## Key Features

- **Lowest PSF in cohort** (MYR 625) combined with **large unit sizes** (2,081 sf+) — creates compelling absolute value for owner-occupiers seeking space
- Freehold tenure; Mont Kiara address
- Bon Estates is a specialist Mont Kiara / North Kiara niche developer — local expertise
- Units up to 6,226 sf suggests duplexes or sky villa units at the top
- The MYR 1.3M entry price (2,081 sf) positions Bon Kiara as the best "space per ringgit" in the cohort

---

## Competitive Positioning

Bon Kiara's 90% take-up at the cohort's lowest PSF is an important insight: in the Mont Kiara large-unit market, buyers respond strongly to value (PSF) rather than brand premium alone. Compare this with Papyrus North Kiara (MYR 675 PSF, 46% take-up) — the 7% higher PSF and lower unit size range of Papyrus produced dramatically worse absorption, suggesting Bon Kiara's product mix is better calibrated to what buyers actually want.

---

## Risks & Watch Points

- **~41 remaining units**: Likely the very large (5,000–6,226 sf) units which represent harder-to-move inventory
- **Niche developer risk**: Bon Estates is a small developer; delivery capacity and post-handover service are less well-proven than tier-1 peers
- **Large unit liquidity**: At sale and at exit — 6,226 sf condos have a thin secondary market globally; resale timelines will be longer

---

## Sources

- [[sources/2025-07-17-urbanmetry-damansara-heights-residential]]

notes.title: botanik-point
notes.content:
# Botanik Point — Urban Hallmark Properties

## Overview

A deliberate ultra-low-density boutique development by Urban Hallmark Properties in Taman Ulu Kelang, Ampang, launching June 2026. The project contains only 46 units — 4 semi-detached homes (>5,500 sf, with private lifts) and 42 Townvillas — on a site approved for up to 108 units. The developer capped supply at 46 to underwrite a nature-centric positioning around a preserved nature reserve that occupies approximately 30% of the site.

Featured in Henry Butcher's 2Q26 newsletter as a highlighted launch — marketed exclusively by Henry Butcher Malaysia.

---

## Unit Mix

| Type | Units | Built-Up (SF) | Key Feature |
|------|-------|---------------|-------------|
| Semi-Detached | 4 | >5,500 | Private lift; premium product |
| Townvilla | 42 | TBC | Boutique low-rise; nature reserve views |

**Total**: 46 units | PSF: not publicly disclosed

---

## Sales Status

- Status: **Pre-launch** — expected June 2026
- Exclusive marketing agent: **Henry Butcher Malaysia**
- Expected completion: 36 months from launch (i.e. ~June 2029)
- Pricing not publicly disclosed as at April 2026

---

## Key Features

**Sustainability & Green**
- GreenRE Platinum (Provisional) certified — one of the highest sustainability ratings for Malaysian residential
- Solar energy generation; rainwater harvesting systems; EV charging provision
- ~30% of site preserved as nature reserve — not developed despite approval for 108 units

**Design**
- Passive ventilation strategy; natural skylights and airwells
- Expansive glass façades; biophilic design language
- Semi-D units include private lifts — rare for the Ampang / Ulu Kelang submarket

**Connectivity**
- 400m to MRR2 (Middle Ring Road 2)
- Near DUKE and AKLEH expressways
- Melawati Mall, KL East Mall, and international schools within short drive distance

**Location Context**
- Taman Ulu Kelang sits at the base of the Titiwangsa Range — permanent greenbelt to the north constrains future supply
- MPAJ jurisdiction (Selangor side of Ampang)

---

## Competitive Positioning

Botanik Point has no direct competitor in the Ulu Kelang / Taman Permata submarket at this specification level. The closest comparable positioning is the boutique-nature concept seen in Eco World developments but at smaller scale. The deliberate supply cap (46 vs 108 approved) signals premium pricing intent — PSF will likely be above the RM 400–700 mid-range Ulu Kelang norm, though Henry Butcher has not published price guidance.

The 4 semi-D units with private lifts are a unique product — no direct comp in this specific submarket.

---

## Risks & Watch Points

- **Pricing opacity**: PSF not disclosed — if pricing is set aggressively high for Ulu Kelang submarket, take-up risk increases
- **Hillside location**: Taman Ulu Kelang is on hilly terrain; slope stability and drainage are standard due diligence items
- **Developer track record**: Urban Hallmark Properties is a niche/boutique developer — less publicly known track record vs tier-1 peers
- **Market segment**: Semi-D with private lifts is a thin buyer pool in the MPAJ / Ulu Kelang context; may suit specific upgrade buyer from the area rather than a KL city buyer

---

## Sources

- [[sources/2026-04-28-hb-advisor-2q26]]

notes.title: impression-u-thant
notes.content:
# Impression U-Thant — Yong Tai

## Overview

A 10-storey boutique residential block by Yong Tai in the U-Thant / Embassy Row corridor. Compact-to-mid-size unit offering (931–1,632 sf) targeting the luxury leasehold buyer and expat renter segment. One of the more successful recent launches in the corridor at 95% take-up. Completing December 2025.

Note: Impression U-Thant is a distinct project from Yong Tai's other project Noble Crest in the same corridor.

---

## Unit Mix

| Type | Size Range (SF) | PSF (MYR) | Notes |
|------|-----------------|-----------|-------|
| Compact | 931 | 1,300–1,500 | 1–2 bedroom range |
| Mid | ~1,200–1,600 | 1,300–1,500 | 2–3 bedroom range |
| Large | 1,632 | 1,300–1,500 | Top of range |

*Detailed unit type breakdown not available in current sources.*

---

## Sales Status

- **Take-up: 95%** (as at early 2026, per BRDB competitor survey)
- 108 units total; approx. 103 units sold
- Completing December 2025 — buyers in final handover / defect rectification phase
- Construction note: Site uses various China contractors (noted as a watch point for nearby BRDB project)

---

## Key Features

- Low-rise boutique character (10 storeys, 1 block) in a predominantly low-density embassy corridor
- Freehold tenure — core selling point in U-Thant market
- Maintenance fee: MYR 0.77 PSF (highest among the surveyed ongoing projects — reflects boutique service level)
- Compact units well-suited to expat tenant demand (931 sf starts at the lower range of the luxury segment)

---

## Competitive Positioning

Among the three actively-selling projects in the corridor as at early 2026, Impression U-Thant occupies the compact-luxury niche. Its 95% take-up at MYR 1,300–1,500 PSF validates demand at this size/price point. Higher maintenance fee vs peers (0.77 vs 0.39–0.50) may affect rental yield calculations.

---

## Risks & Watch Points

- **Near-completion execution**: Defect rectification and VP process risks for buyers
- **Rental competition**: As Skyline Embassy (1,296 units) completes in 2027, rental supply in the compact segment will increase, which may pressure Impression U-Thant's resale and rental performance
- **China contractor reputation**: Site construction quality by China sub-contractors noted as a watch point in BRDB's competitor analysis

---

## Sources

- [[sources/2026-04-28-brdb-u-thant-marketing-brief]]

notes.title: jendela-klgcc
notes.content:
# Jendela @ KLGCC — Sime Darby Property

## Overview

A leasehold, commercial-title residential project by Sime Darby Property within the KL Golf & Country Club (KLGCC) precinct in Damansara Heights. 520 units ranging 1,324–2,863 sf at a nett starting price of MYR 1.25M / MYR 947 PSF. Near-fully absorbed at 98% SPA signed — one of the highest take-up rates in the Damansara Heights competitor set.

The KLGCC precinct benefits from the established golf club and resort environment, though leasehold + commercial title creates a pricing and financing discount vs freehold residential peers.

---

## Unit Mix

| Size Range (SF) | Nett Start PSF (MYR) | Notes |
|-----------------|----------------------|-------|
| 1,324–2,863 | ~947 | Leasehold commercial; golf course precinct |

*Detailed unit type breakdown not available in current sources.*

---

## Sales Status

- **Take-up: 98% SPA signed** (UrbanMetry data, as at mid-2025)
- 520 units; approx. 510 units sold
- Near-fully absorbed — validates demand for the KLGCC precinct at this price point

---

## Key Features

- Located within the prestigious KL Golf & Country Club precinct
- Leasehold + commercial title: lower entry point PSF vs freehold residential peers
- Golf course and resort facility access for residents
- Part of Sime Darby Property's KLGCC estate development alongside The Ophera

---

## Competitive Positioning

At MYR 947 PSF with 98% take-up, Jendela achieved the highest absorption in the competitor set at the study date. The leasehold/commercial title discount (vs freehold residential peers at MYR 1,199–1,599 PSF) explains the PSF gap. Investors likely driven by KLGCC brand and lower absolute entry price relative to freehold alternatives.

---

## Risks & Watch Points

- **Leasehold tenure**: Remaining lease term will shorten over time; bank financing terms and resale value impacted
- **Commercial title**: Higher maintenance costs (commercial assessment rates), some financing restrictions
- **Sime Darby delivery**: Large developer with good track record; delivery risk is low but KLGCC precinct projects are complex

---

## Sources

- [[sources/2025-07-17-urbanmetry-damansara-heights-residential]]

notes.title: kiaramas-dedaun-ph1
notes.content:
# Kiaramas Dedaun Phase 1 — Asia Quest Holdings

## Overview

Phase 1 of Asia Quest Holdings' Kiaramas Dedaun project in Mont Kiara. 436 freehold residential units ranging 1,313–4,693 sf at a nett starting PSF of MYR 784. 68% SPA signed — a respectable absorption rate for the Mont Kiara market. Asia Quest Holdings is a niche developer active in the Mont Kiara / North Kiara corridor.

Kiaramas Dedaun is a two-phase project: Phase 1 (436 units, 68% take-up) has better absorption than Phase 2 (223 units, 31% take-up), which is expected given Phase 2 was likely launched later.

---

## Unit Mix

| Size Range (SF) | Nett Start PSF (MYR) | Notes |
|-----------------|----------------------|-------|
| 1,313–4,693 | ~784 | Mixed compact-to-large; freehold Mont Kiara |

*Detailed unit type breakdown not available in current sources.*

---

## Sales Status

- **Take-up: 68% SPA signed** (UrbanMetry data, as at mid-2025)
- 436 units; approx. 296 units sold
- Better absorption than Phase 2 (31%) — Phase 1 early buyer incentive likely contributed

---

## Key Features

- Freehold tenure in Mont Kiara — meaningful differentiator
- Wide size range accommodates entry-level investor (1,313 sf) through to owner-occupier (4,693 sf)
- Asia Quest Holdings is a specialised Mont Kiara corridor developer with local market knowledge
- "Dedaun" branding connotes nature/greenery — consistent with the treetop/canopy aesthetic seen in Mont Kiara premium launches

---

## Competitive Positioning

At MYR 784 PSF, Kiaramas Dedaun Ph1 sits between Papyrus (MYR 675) and Pavilion Mont Kiara (MYR 1,199). The 68% take-up is the third-best in the 12-project cohort (after KLGCC Jendela 98% and Pavilion Mont Kiara 99%), suggesting the MYR 784 PSF / MYR 1M starting price is a sweet spot for the Mont Kiara buyer.

---

## Risks & Watch Points

- **Phase 2 drag**: Phase 2 at 31% take-up (same project, same developer) suggests late-buyer demand has dried up for this precinct at this price — a signal that absorption may slow for unsold Ph1 units too
- **Mont Kiara oversupply**: The corridor has high incoming supply; competition for the same buyer pool is intense

---

## Sources

- [[sources/2025-07-17-urbanmetry-damansara-heights-residential]]

notes.title: kiaramas-dedaun-ph2
notes.content:
# Kiaramas Dedaun Phase 2 — Asia Quest Holdings

## Overview

Phase 2 of Asia Quest Holdings' Kiaramas Dedaun project in Mont Kiara. 223 freehold residential units ranging 1,313–2,777 sf at a nett starting PSF of MYR 797. Take-up is only 31% SPA signed — the weakest absorption in the 12-project cohort alongside The Ophera (47%) and Papyrus North Kiara (46%). Contrast with Phase 1's 68% take-up illustrates early-buyer depletion and a cooling Mont Kiara market.

---

## Unit Mix

| Size Range (SF) | Nett Start PSF (MYR) | Notes |
|-----------------|----------------------|-------|
| 1,313–2,777 | ~797 | Compact-to-mid; freehold Mont Kiara; Phase 2 |

*Detailed unit type breakdown not available in current sources.*

---

## Sales Status

- **Take-up: 31% SPA signed** (UrbanMetry data, as at mid-2025)
- 223 units; approx. 69 units sold — lowest absolute number sold among the cohort
- Significantly below Phase 1's 68% — later launch timing + exhausted early buyer pool

---

## Key Features

- Freehold; same Kiaramas Dedaun precinct and branding as Phase 1
- Slightly smaller maximum unit size vs Phase 1 (2,777 vs 4,693 sf) — Phase 2 likely targets different buyer
- Marginally higher PSF (MYR 797 vs MYR 784) — typical phase escalation

---

## Competitive Positioning

Phase 2's 31% take-up is a red flag for the Mont Kiara mid-market. It suggests that early adopter/investor demand was concentrated in Phase 1, and the broader market at MYR 797 PSF with this developer/product has limited pull. Developers launching sequential phases need to carefully monitor take-up depletion.

---

## Risks & Watch Points

- **Slow absorption**: 69% of units still unsold — developer likely holding inventory or considering repricing/incentives
- **Phase 1 overhang**: If Phase 1 investors seek exit before Phase 2 is complete, the secondary market will compete with Phase 2 primary sales
- **Mont Kiara supply pressure**: Incoming pipeline is large relative to annual organic demand

---

## Sources

- [[sources/2025-07-17-urbanmetry-damansara-heights-residential]]

notes.title: miranda-hill
notes.content:
# Miranda Hill — BRDB

## Overview

A freehold residential project by BRDB (Bandar Raya Developments) in Mont Kiara / Bukit Damansara area. 552 units ranging 943–2,200 sf at a nett starting PSF of MYR 697. 89% SPA signed — strong absorption for the project's price band, making Miranda Hill one of BRDB's well-performing mid-tier offerings.

Note: BRDB simultaneously has the ultra-luxury U-Thant (Langgak Golf) project at ~MYR 1,500 PSF in the pipeline — Miranda Hill represents BRDB's mid-market tier vs the boutique ultra-premium tier.

---

## Unit Mix

| Size Range (SF) | Nett Start PSF (MYR) | Notes |
|-----------------|----------------------|-------|
| 943–2,200 | ~697 | Compact to mid-large; freehold |

*Detailed unit type breakdown not available in current sources.*

---

## Sales Status

- **Take-up: 89% SPA signed** (UrbanMetry data, as at mid-2025)
- 552 units; approx. 491 units sold
- Solid absorption at the MYR 657k–1.53M price point — BRDB's sweet spot for volume sales in this corridor

---

## Key Features

- Freehold tenure; established BRDB branding (known for D'Sara Heights legacy projects)
- Compact starting size (943 sf) creates accessible entry point for investors; large end (2,200 sf) captures owner-occupiers
- BRDB's mid-market positioning before pivoting to ultra-luxury (Langgak Golf)

---

## Competitive Positioning

Miranda Hill at MYR 697 PSF sits near the bottom of the cohort alongside Bon Kiara (MYR 625) and Papyrus (MYR 675). Yet the 89% take-up is the fourth-best in the cohort — significantly above Papyrus (46%) and Kiaramas Dedaun Ph2 (31%). BRDB's brand and BRDB's legacy in the Bukit Damansara / D'Sara Heights area likely drives the absorption advantage over peers at similar PSF.

---

## Risks & Watch Points

- **BRDB brand stretch**: Simultaneously managing Miranda Hill (MYR 697 PSF) and Langgak Golf (~MYR 1,500 PSF) requires careful brand segmentation — risk of brand confusion if Langgak Golf does not launch successfully
- **Remaining ~61 unsold units**: Mostly likely large units or less favourable stacks

---

## Sources

- [[sources/2025-07-17-urbanmetry-damansara-heights-residential]]

notes.title: papyrus-north-kiara
notes.content:
# Papyrus North Kiara — Kerjaya Prospek

## Overview

A freehold residential project by Kerjaya Prospek in the North Kiara / Mont Kiara corridor. 454 units ranging from compact 1,141 sf to very large 5,523 sf at a nett starting PSF of MYR 675. Take-up is 46% — below the cohort average, reflecting the challenging mid-range Mont Kiara market where high incoming supply and competitive pressure from Damansara Heights and other submarkets weigh on absorption.

Kerjaya Prospek is primarily known as a construction contractor (one of Malaysia's strongest); this is a developer-side project, which is relatively less common for the group.

---

## Unit Mix

| Size Range (SF) | Nett Start PSF (MYR) | Notes |
|-----------------|----------------------|-------|
| 1,141–5,523 | ~675 | Wide range; freehold; North Kiara |

*Detailed unit type breakdown not available in current sources.*

---

## Sales Status

- **Take-up: 46% SPA signed** (UrbanMetry data, as at mid-2025)
- 454 units; approx. 209 units sold
- Below-cohort absorption; supply-demand dynamics in Mont Kiara / North Kiara are challenging

---

## Key Features

- Wide unit size range accommodates multiple buyer segments (compact investor to large owner-occupier)
- Freehold tenure — a differentiator in the leasehold-heavy KLGCC alternatives
- Kerjaya Prospek's construction pedigree may translate to build quality advantage
- North Kiara location: slightly north of the established Mont Kiara core; some buyers may view as peripheral

---

## Competitive Positioning

At MYR 675 PSF, Papyrus is the second-cheapest in the 12-project cohort (above only Bon Kiara at MYR 625). The low PSF reflects the North Kiara location premium discount vs Bukit Damansara (MYR 1,429–1,599 PSF). The 46% take-up is average for the Mont Kiara segment but below the cohort leaders.

---

## Risks & Watch Points

- **North Kiara location**: Not in the Bukit Damansara enclave; may struggle to achieve the PSF uplift that justifies the large-unit price tag
- **Supply competition**: KLGCC Jendela at 98% take-up (MYR 947 PSF) shows buyers prefer the KLGCC brand at higher PSF — implies location/brand matters more than PSF at this price band
- **Developer track record as developer** (vs contractor): Less established as a residential developer; managing completions and handover quality at developer scale is a different muscle

---

## Sources

- [[sources/2025-07-17-urbanmetry-damansara-heights-residential]]

notes.title: pavilion-damansara-heights
notes.content:
# Pavilion Damansara Heights (Phase 1) — WCT Land / Pavilion

## Overview

A major mixed-use development in Damansara Heights completed in 2023. Phase 1 comprises **Pavilion Damansara Heights mall** (533,361 sf NLA retail), strata office towers, and residential components. Part of the WCT Land and Pavilion group's integrated mixed-use strategy in the Damansara Heights corridor.

---

## Retail Performance (as at mid-2025)

- **NLA**: 533,361 sf
- **Occupancy**: 53% — still ramping up 2 years after opening
- **Monthly footfall**: 121,650 — early stage; below mature mall benchmarks
- **Status**: Stabilising but underperforming relative to market expectations

The retail underperformance (53% at 2 years) is a concern noted in UrbanMetry's analysis. The broader Damansara Heights study area has 7.4 sf of retail per capita (above Singapore's 5.8 sf) — structural oversupply.

---

## Strata Office Component

- Pavilion Bukit Damansara Heights towers: MYR 7.50 PSF asking rent — highest in the study area
- Strata office yields: ~4.5% at current prices (vs ~6% for older stock below MYR 850 PSF)

---

## Risks & Watch Points

- **Retail ramp-up**: 53% occupancy at 2 years is below comparable mall benchmarks; leasing still in progress
- **Retail supply competition**: Significant additional retail NLA incoming in the corridor
- **Brand dependency**: Pavilion brand raised buyer/tenant expectations — performance gap risks brand dilution

---

## Sources

- [[sources/2025-07-17-urbanmetry-damansara-heights-residential]]
- [[sources/2025-07-17-urbanmetry-damansara-heights-retail-offices]]

notes.title: pavilion-mont-kiara
notes.content:
# Pavilion Mont Kiara — WCT Land

## Overview

A near-fully sold freehold residential project by WCT Land in Mont Kiara, leveraging the Pavilion brand (via WCT's partnership with the Pavilion group). 336 units ranging 1,001–2,530 sf at a premium PSF of MYR 1,199 — the highest PSF achieved in the Mont Kiara cohort (excluding Bukit Damansara projects). 99% SPA signed — the second-best take-up in the 12-project cohort, behind only KLGCC Jendela.

The Pavilion brand adds a meaningful premium to what would otherwise be a standard Mont Kiara mid-rise project.

---

## Unit Mix

| Size Range (SF) | Nett Start PSF (MYR) | Notes |
|-----------------|----------------------|-------|
| 1,001–2,530 | ~1,199 | 2–3 bedroom range; brand-premium |

*Detailed unit type breakdown not available in current sources.*

---

## Sales Status

- **Take-up: 99% SPA signed** (UrbanMetry data, as at mid-2025)
- 336 units; approx. 333 units sold — virtually fully absorbed
- Demonstrates that brand premium (Pavilion) plus freehold + right size range can command the highest PSF in Mont Kiara with strong absorption

---

## Key Features

- **Pavilion brand**: One of Malaysia's most recognised luxury retail/property brands; significant buyer confidence uplift
- Freehold tenure; Mont Kiara address
- Size range (1,001–2,530 sf) covers the mainstream owner-occupier sweet spot in the corridor
- WCT Land is an established developer with mixed commercial-residential experience

---

## Competitive Positioning

Pavilion Mont Kiara is the clearest example in the study of brand premium driving both PSF uplift and absorption simultaneously. At MYR 1,199 PSF — 24% above the next-highest PSF project (The Minh at MYR 809) in the Mont Kiara / North Kiara cluster — and 99% take-up, it shows that branding is more powerful than pricing in the right segment.

This has implications for the BRDB U-Thant Langgak Golf project: brand-led positioning can sustain premium PSF if the concept is strong enough.

---

## Risks & Watch Points

- **Brand dependency**: The premium may not sustain at resale if broader Mont Kiara market weakens
- **WCT delivery quality**: WCT is a mid-tier developer; build quality and post-handover service will need to match the Pavilion brand expectation
- **1 unit still unsold**: Likely a penthouse or large unit that may require time to move

---

## Sources

- [[sources/2025-07-17-urbanmetry-damansara-heights-residential]]

notes.title: sdb-damansara-condo
notes.content:
# SDB Damansara Condo — SDB (Selangor Dredging Berhad)

## Overview

A proposed residential development by SDB in the Damansara Heights landed enclave. 60 units planned. Status: **unlikely to proceed**.

The project faces a fundamental planning impasse: the site is gazetted for a maximum of **3 storeys**, while SDB has requested **7 storeys** from the planning authority. UrbanMetry notes this as a project unlikely to proceed given the gazette constraint vs developer's commercial aspiration.

SDB (Selangor Dredging Berhad) is a listed Malaysian developer (Bursa: 1783) with a track record in niche KL residential (e.g. Ara Damansara, Sierramas West).

---

## Sales Status

- Status: **Cancelled / Unlikely to proceed** — gazette constraint (max 3s) vs developer request (7s)

---

## Open Questions

- Has there been any planning authority decision or appeal filed?
- Could SDB scale down to 3 storeys (landed bungalow/villa product) and still achieve commercial viability?

---

## Sources

- [[sources/2025-07-17-urbanmetry-damansara-heights-residential]]

notes.title: skyline-embassy
notes.content:
# Skyline Embassy — TS Law Land

## Overview

A large-scale 65-storey, 3-block high-rise residential development by TS Law Land, located beside the former British High Commission land at the Jalan Ampang / Tun Razak end of the Embassy Row corridor. With 1,296 units of compact (521–976 sf) freehold apartments at MYR 1,350 PSF, this is the mass-market high-rise counterpart to the boutique low-rise segment in the U-Thant corridor. Completing 2027.

The project leverages LRT connectivity (Ampang Park) and proximity to Linc and Intermark retail strip. Its scale and location put it in a different sub-segment from boutique low-rise peers.

---

## Unit Mix

| Size Range (SF) | PSF (MYR) | Notes |
|-----------------|-----------|-------|
| 521–976 | 1,350 | Compact studio to 2-bed; investor-grade rental product |

*Detailed unit type breakdown not available in current sources.*

---

## Sales Status

- **Take-up: 95%** (as at early 2026, per BRDB competitor survey)
- 1,296 units total; approx. 1,231 units sold
- Completing 2027 — significant rental supply addition when completed

---

## Key Features

- High-rise scale: 65 storeys, 3 blocks — one of the tallest in the immediate corridor
- Location advantage: LRT Ampang Park, Linc KL, Intermark Mall within walking distance
- Compact units well-matched to the rental market (studio to 2-bed)
- Maintenance fee: MYR 0.50 PSF — lower than boutique peers, consistent with a larger development
- Freehold tenure

---

## Competitive Positioning

Skyline Embassy and the boutique low-rise projects in U-Thant serve fundamentally different buyer segments. Skyline targets the investor/compact-rental buyer at sub-MYR 1M total price; the boutique low-rise segment (Ashwood, Impression, Langgak Golf) targets owner-occupiers and HNW buyers at MYR 1.2M–6.4M. The 95% take-up confirms strong investor appetite for compact high-rise in this corridor at the right PSF.

**Key watch:** 1,296 units completing in 2027 will meaningfully increase rental supply in the compact segment and could compress yields for Impression U-Thant owners and future Skyline Embassy resale investors.

---

## Risks & Watch Points

- **Rental supply shock**: When 1,296 units enter the rental market in 2027, vacancy rates for the compact segment (521–976 sf) in the corridor will likely rise temporarily
- **Resale competition**: Large block means a permanent secondary market inventory overhang
- **Developer track record**: TS Law Land is a less established developer — delivery risk and post-handover service quality are watch points

---

## Sources

- [[sources/2026-04-28-brdb-u-thant-marketing-brief]]

notes.title: the-ashwood
notes.content:
# The Ashwood — Paramount

## Overview

A 49-storey, 2-block luxury high-rise condominium by Paramount in the U-Thant / Embassy Row corridor. The Ashwood targets the mid-large unit segment (1,400–3,000 sf) at MYR 1,200–1,300 PSF and has achieved 100% take-up — the strongest absorption of any ongoing project in the corridor. Completing 2029.

Note: Paramount also developed The Atrium in Jalan Ampang Hilir, which is a completed project in the same broader corridor.

---

## Unit Mix

| Size Range (SF) | PSF (MYR) | Notes |
|-----------------|-----------|-------|
| 1,400–3,000 | 1,200–1,300 | 2–4 bedroom range; large-format luxury |

*Detailed unit type breakdown not available in current sources.*

---

## Sales Status

- **Take-up: 100%** — fully sold out (as at early 2026)
- 354 units; all SPAs signed
- Under construction, completing 2029
- Benchmark for demand validation in the 1,400–3,000 sf, MYR 1.2M–3.9M price band

---

## Key Features

- Two 49-storey towers — taller than Impression U-Thant (10s) but lower density than Skyline Embassy (65s, 3 blocks)
- Large unit format validates owner-occupier demand in the corridor at moderate (relative to boutique) density
- Maintenance fee: MYR 0.39 PSF — lowest of the active ongoing projects, notable for a premium development
- Freehold tenure; Jalan U-Thant location within core embassy district

---

## Competitive Positioning

The Ashwood's 100% sell-out at MYR 1,200–1,300 PSF for units 1,400–3,000 sf is the clearest demand evidence in the corridor. It demonstrates that the buyer pool for mid-large luxury units in the U-Thant belt is robust. BRDB's Langgak Golf pricing (~MYR 1,500 PSF at 2,080–5,000 sf) targets a step up in PSF and scale from The Ashwood.

---

## Risks & Watch Points

- **Delivery risk (2029)**: Four years from launch to expected completion — macro, material cost, and labour risks
- **Resale market**: 354 units completing in 2029 will add supply to the subsale market simultaneously; investor resales could compete with each other
- **Premium maintenance fee compression**: Low fee (0.39 PSF) may be introductory and subject to upward revision post-management by residents' JMB/MC

---

## Sources

- [[sources/2026-04-28-brdb-u-thant-marketing-brief]]
